channel.title: The New Beverly Cinema
channel.description: Schedule and program for The New Beverly Cinema (Quentin Tarantino's revival theater in Los Angeles).

item.title: Behind the Green Door (Adults Only)
item.description:
As part of a strange club’s elaborate sexual performance, anonymous lovers emerge through a mysterious green door to fulfill a woman’s deepest longings and hidden desires. A low budget, but highly ambitious effort which would become one of the biggest X-rated hits of the 1970s, the Mitchell Brothers’ landmark Behind the Green Door made an international sensation and bonafide star out of its leading lady Marilyn Chambers, while also becoming one of the first hardcore features to win mainstream acclaim for both its technical innovation and artistic merits.
Cast: Director: Jim and Artie Mitchell | Starring: Marilyn Chambers, Johnny Keyes, George McDonald, Ben Davidson | Year: 1972 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 72 minuti

Orari: Sat March 28 - 11:59 pm

item.title: True Romance
item.description:
A rock ‘n roll adventure following two unlikely lovers who accidentally double cross the mob and hit the road towards Los Angeles. Featuring an all-star cast, slick direction from Tony Scott and an endlessly-quotable script by Quentin Tarantino, True Romance will have you saying “you’re so cool” all month long. “This is the kind of movie that creates its own universe, and glories in it. Made with such energy, such high spirits, such an enchanting goofiness, that it’s impossible to resist.” – Roger Ebert “It’s Tarantino’s gutter poetry that detonates True Romance . This movie is dynamite.” – Peter Travers, Rolling Stone
Cast: Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Christian Slater, Patricia Arquette, Dennis Hopper, Val Kilmer, Gary Oldman, Brad Pitt, Christopher Walken, Bronson Pinchot, Samuel L. Jackson, Michael Rapaport, Saul Rubinek, Conchata Ferrell and James Gandolfini | Year: 1993 | Country: USA/France | Format: 35mm
Durata: 119 minuti

Orari: Fri March 27 - 11:59 pm

item.title: The Hot Box / Terminal Island
item.description:
Award-winning filmmaker Jonathan Demme ( The Silence of the Lambs ) produces and co-writes The Hot Box , an action-thriller straight from the grimiest corner of the grindhouse. Four nurses working in the tropical Republic of San Rosario enter a hellish nightmare when they are kidnapped and delivered to a rebel army fighting a guerilla war against oppressive government forces. Tormented by every man that crosses their path, the four women must band together in the dense jungle in pursuit of a solitary goal: survival. (Shout! Factory) A cult movie slayer from acclaimed filmmaker Stephanie Rothman ( The Velvet Vampire ), Terminal Island blends violent action with commentary on the American justice system. Carmen Simms has just been sentenced to life imprisonment on Terminal Island – an island created to house vicious criminals in the wake of prison overcrowding. With no guards, no cells, and no laws, Terminal Island has morphed into a defacto society with the tyrannical Bobby ruling over all. As tension and violence mount, Carmen finds her place as the unlikely leader of the island’s underground resistance, which is planning to overthrow Bobby’s reign of terror. Starring Phyllis Davis ( Sweet Sugar ) and Emmy winner Tom Selleck (Magnum P.I.) in one of his earliest roles. (American Genre Film Archive)
Cast: Director: Joe Viola | Writer: Joe Viola and Jonathan Demme | Starring: Andrea Cagan, Margaret Markov, Rickey Richardson, Laurie Rose, Carmen Argenziano, Charles Dierkop | Year: 1972 | Country: Philippines/USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Stephanie Rothman | Writer: Written by Jim Barnett and Charles S. Swartz & Stephanie Rothman | Starring: Phyllis Davis, Don Marshall, Ena Hartman, Marta Kristen, Barbara Leigh, Tom Selleck, Roger E. Mosley | Year: 1973 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 88 minuti

Orari: Sat March 28 - 6:30 pm / 8:30 pm

item.title: Lady Frankenstein / Slaughter Hotel
item.description:
When Dr. Frankenstein is killed by his monster, his daughter Tania and lab assistant Marshall continue his experiments. As the monster rampages across the European countryside, an unexpected romance blossoms between the two and they begin to scheme to put the aging Marshall’s brain inside the body of their brawny, but dimwitted servant’s body. Co-produced by Roger Corman and with an international cast of cult favorites including Joseph Cotten ( Citizen Kane ), Rosalba Neri ( The Devil’s Lover ), and Paul Müller ( Nightmare Castle ), Lady Frankenstein is an ultra-lurid and underseen classic of Italian Gothic. (American Genre Film Archive) Schedule change: Please note that our previously announced screening of Amuck has been cancelled due to a print-related issue, and is being replaced by Lady Frankenstein. Cult icons Klaus Kinski, Margaret Lee, and Rosalba Neri in a sex-filled thriller from director Fernando Di Leo! Fernando Di Leo, best known for a series of crime films, tried his hand at horror with this extremely sick little item loaded with nudity and violence. Set at a remote mental institution (inexplicably located in a castle), the film features Klaus Kinski as a doctor whose mostly female patients are being brutally dispatched by a psychopath. Margaret Lee and Rosalba Neri are among the frequently unclothed cast. (Kino Lorber)
Cast: Director: Mel Welles | Starring: Joseph Cotten, Rosalba Neri (as Sarah Bay), Mickey Hargitay, Paul Muller | Year: 1971 | Country: Italy | Format: 16mm | Director: Fernando Di Leo | Writer: Screenplay & Story by Fernando Di Leo & Nino Latino | Starring: Klaus Kinski, Margaret Lee, Rosalba Neri, Jane Garret | Year: 1971 | Country: Italy | Format: 35mm
Durata: 91 minuti

Orari: Thu March 26 - 7:30 pm / 9:25 pm

item.title: Lady Frankenstein / Slaughter Hotel
item.description:
When Dr. Frankenstein is killed by his monster, his daughter Tania and lab assistant Marshall continue his experiments. As the monster rampages across the European countryside, an unexpected romance blossoms between the two and they begin to scheme to put the aging Marshall’s brain inside the body of their brawny, but dimwitted servant’s body. Co-produced by Roger Corman and with an international cast of cult favorites including Joseph Cotten ( Citizen Kane ), Rosalba Neri ( The Devil’s Lover ), and Paul Müller ( Nightmare Castle ), Lady Frankenstein is an ultra-lurid and underseen classic of Italian Gothic. (American Genre Film Archive) Schedule change: Please note that our previously announced screening of Amuck has been cancelled due to a print-related issue, and is being replaced by Lady Frankenstein. Cult icons Klaus Kinski, Margaret Lee, and Rosalba Neri in a sex-filled thriller from director Fernando Di Leo! Fernando Di Leo, best known for a series of crime films, tried his hand at horror with this extremely sick little item loaded with nudity and violence. Set at a remote mental institution (inexplicably located in a castle), the film features Klaus Kinski as a doctor whose mostly female patients are being brutally dispatched by a psychopath. Margaret Lee and Rosalba Neri are among the frequently unclothed cast. (Kino Lorber)
Cast: Director: Mel Welles | Starring: Joseph Cotten, Rosalba Neri (as Sarah Bay), Mickey Hargitay, Paul Muller | Year: 1971 | Country: Italy | Format: 16mm | Director: Fernando Di Leo | Writer: Screenplay & Story by Fernando Di Leo & Nino Latino | Starring: Klaus Kinski, Margaret Lee, Rosalba Neri, Jane Garret | Year: 1971 | Country: Italy | Format: 35mm
Durata: 91 minuti

Orari: Fri March 27 - 6:30 pm / 8:25 pm

item.title: Last Tango in Paris
item.description:
Paul, a middle-aged American living in emotional exile, comes to Paris when his estranged wife commits suicide. Chancing to meet young Frenchwoman named Jeanne, Paul enters into a sadomasochistic, carnal relationship with her, indirectly attacking the hypocrisy all around him and his own feelings of guilt through his raw, outrageous sexual behaviour. (Park Circus)
Cast: Director: Bernardo Bertolucci | Starring: Marlon Brando, Maria Schneider, Maria Michi, Giovanna Galletti, Jean-Pierre Léaud, Massimo Girotti | Year: 1972 | Country: Italy/France | Format: 35mm
Durata: 129 minuti

Orari: Tue March 31 - 7:30 pm

item.title: The Hot Box / Terminal Island
item.description:
Award-winning filmmaker Jonathan Demme ( The Silence of the Lambs ) produces and co-writes The Hot Box , an action-thriller straight from the grimiest corner of the grindhouse. Four nurses working in the tropical Republic of San Rosario enter a hellish nightmare when they are kidnapped and delivered to a rebel army fighting a guerilla war against oppressive government forces. Tormented by every man that crosses their path, the four women must band together in the dense jungle in pursuit of a solitary goal: survival. (Shout! Factory) A cult movie slayer from acclaimed filmmaker Stephanie Rothman ( The Velvet Vampire ), Terminal Island blends violent action with commentary on the American justice system. Carmen Simms has just been sentenced to life imprisonment on Terminal Island – an island created to house vicious criminals in the wake of prison overcrowding. With no guards, no cells, and no laws, Terminal Island has morphed into a defacto society with the tyrannical Bobby ruling over all. As tension and violence mount, Carmen finds her place as the unlikely leader of the island’s underground resistance, which is planning to overthrow Bobby’s reign of terror. Starring Phyllis Davis ( Sweet Sugar ) and Emmy winner Tom Selleck (Magnum P.I.) in one of his earliest roles. (American Genre Film Archive)
Cast: Director: Joe Viola | Writer: Joe Viola and Jonathan Demme | Starring: Andrea Cagan, Margaret Markov, Rickey Richardson, Laurie Rose, Carmen Argenziano, Charles Dierkop | Year: 1972 | Country: Philippines/USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Stephanie Rothman | Writer: Written by Jim Barnett and Charles S. Swartz & Stephanie Rothman | Starring: Phyllis Davis, Don Marshall, Ena Hartman, Marta Kristen, Barbara Leigh, Tom Selleck, Roger E. Mosley | Year: 1973 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 88 minuti

Orari: Sun March 29 - 6:30 pm / 8:30 pm

item.title: Last Tango in Paris
item.description:
Paul, a middle-aged American living in emotional exile, comes to Paris when his estranged wife commits suicide. Chancing to meet young Frenchwoman named Jeanne, Paul enters into a sadomasochistic, carnal relationship with her, indirectly attacking the hypocrisy all around him and his own feelings of guilt through his raw, outrageous sexual behaviour. (Park Circus)
Cast: Director: Bernardo Bertolucci | Starring: Marlon Brando, Maria Schneider, Maria Michi, Giovanna Galletti, Jean-Pierre Léaud, Massimo Girotti | Year: 1972 | Country: Italy/France | Format: 35mm
Durata: 129 minuti

Orari: Mon March 30 - 7:30 pm
